evals.prompt: Design a REST API for a blog platform. It needs endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting blog posts. Posts have a title, body, author, tags, and published status. Include authentication.
evals.expected_output: A complete OpenAPI 3.1 YAML specification with CRUD paths for posts, concrete schemas, auth, cursor pagination, idempotency where useful, and standard error responses.
expectations: Output starts with or contains openapi: "3.1.0"
CRUD paths for posts are defined
Post, create request, and update request schemas include concrete fields
List endpoint uses cursor-style pagination or explicitly justifies another pagination style
Authentication security scheme is defined
Shared ErrorResponse schema is referenced by error responses

evals.prompt: Design an API for a task management app. Users can create projects, add tasks to projects, assign tasks to team members, and update task status. Use OpenAPI.
evals.expected_output: OpenAPI YAML with project and task resources, assignment and status operations, concrete schemas, enums, path parameters, and errors.
expectations: Output is OpenAPI YAML
Project and Task schemas are defined
Task assignment operation is defined
Task status is represented as an enum
All path parameters include schema definitions
Operations include success and error responses

evals.prompt: Create an AsyncAPI contract for order lifecycle events over Kafka. The service publishes order.created, order.paid, and order.cancelled events. Consumers need customer id, line items, totals, and timestamps.
evals.expected_output: AsyncAPI 2.6 YAML with Kafka server, order lifecycle channels/messages, concrete payload schemas, event envelope, and reliability notes.
expectations: Output starts with or contains asyncapi: "2.6.0"
Kafka server is defined
Channels or messages cover created, paid, and cancelled order events
Payload schemas include customer id, line items, totals, and timestamps
Event metadata includes event id and occurred timestamp
No OpenAPI paths section is used

evals.prompt: Define a GraphQL API for a commerce catalog. Clients need to query products by category, search products, view variants, and create product reviews.
evals.expected_output: GraphQL SDL with Query, Mutation, product/category/variant/review types, inputs, pagination, and concise operation notes.
expectations: Output contains GraphQL SDL rather than OpenAPI YAML
Query type supports product category browsing or search
Mutation type supports creating product reviews
Product, Category, Variant, and Review types are defined
Pagination types or connection pattern are included
Operation notes mention auth or resolver considerations

evals.prompt: What should the API look like for uploading documents, checking processing status, and downloading extracted text? Assume REST unless you strongly disagree.
evals.expected_output: OpenAPI 3.1 YAML for document upload/status/download with multipart upload, status enum, response schemas, errors, and stated REST assumption.
expectations: Output chooses OpenAPI
REST assumption is stated once
Upload endpoint supports multipart or binary content
Processing status schema includes an enum
Download or extracted text retrieval endpoint is defined
Error responses use a shared schema

evals.prompt: Design a WebSocket event API for live shipment tracking. Clients subscribe to location updates, delay alerts, and delivery confirmation events.
evals.expected_output: AsyncAPI 2.6 YAML for WebSocket channels/messages with shipment event payloads, concrete schemas, and subscription direction.
expectations: Output is AsyncAPI
WebSocket protocol is represented
Location update, delay alert, and delivery confirmation messages are defined
Shipment payload schemas include concrete location and status fields
Publish/subscribe direction is clear
No REST CRUD paths are used

evals.prompt: Write the Swagger spec for a billing API with customers, invoices, invoice payment, refunds, and webhook registration. Use API keys.
evals.expected_output: OpenAPI 3.1 YAML with billing resources, API key auth, payment/refund operations, webhook registration, schemas, and errors.
expectations: Output is OpenAPI YAML
API key security scheme is defined
Customer and Invoice schemas are defined
Payment and refund operations are included
Webhook registration path or schema is included
Money fields avoid floating point ambiguity or define decimal handling

evals.prompt: Design an AsyncAPI spec for user account events: user.registered, user.email_verified, user.deleted. Include compatibility guidance for consumers.
evals.expected_output: AsyncAPI 2.6 YAML with account event messages, concrete user payload schemas, envelope metadata, and compatibility notes.
expectations: Output is AsyncAPI YAML
All three user event types are represented
User payload schemas include concrete fields
Event envelope metadata is present
Compatibility or versioning guidance is included
No empty payload schemas are emitted

evals.prompt: Create a GraphQL contract for team collaboration: organizations have teams, teams have members, members can be invited, removed, and assigned roles.
evals.expected_output: GraphQL SDL with organization/team/member types, role enum, invite/remove/assign mutations, inputs, errors, and auth notes.
expectations: Output contains GraphQL SDL
Organization, Team, and Member types are defined
Role enum is defined
Mutations cover invite, removal, and role assignment
Mutation payloads include user errors or equivalent error shape
Auth boundaries are noted

evals.prompt: Design a public API contract for a notification service. It needs REST endpoints for templates and subscriptions, plus events when notifications are delivered or bounced.
evals.expected_output: A mixed contract with OpenAPI for REST resources and AsyncAPI for delivery/bounce events, clearly separated and concrete.
expectations: Output includes both OpenAPI and AsyncAPI sections
REST resources cover templates and subscriptions
Async events cover delivered and bounced notifications
Schemas are concrete and domain-specific
The two contract styles are clearly separated
Shared identifiers are consistent across REST and event payloads
